articles.title: CrossViT with ECAP: Enhanced deep learning for jaw lesion classification
articles.authors: Warin K; et al. (Chiang Mai University, Thailand)
articles.keywords: CrossViT; ECAP; jaw lesion classification; ameloblastoma; OKC; dentigerous cyst; radicular cyst
articles.abstract: Radiolucent jaw lesions (AM, DC, OKC, RC) often share similar characteristics, making diagnosis challenging. This study investigated CrossViT (multi-scale vision transformers) and a novel ECAP (Extended Cropping and Padding) technique against ResNets for classifying 208 lesions in panoramic OPGs. CrossViT-18 achieved the best overall performance. CrossViT models consistently outperformed ResNets regardless of ECAP usage. DC had highest AUCs (0.89–0.90) and OKC the lowest (0.72–0.81). ECAP improves performance for rare lesions with limited data.
